alkylation reagent: alkylation with 11 mM iodoacetamide (IAM) for 15 min at room temperature in darkness.
alkylation concentration: alkylation with 11 mM iodoacetamide (IAM) for 15 min at room temperature in darkness.
cleavage agent: digested at a trypsin-to-protein mass ratio of 1:50 for the first digestion overnight and 1:100 for a 4-h digestion.
fractionation method: the samples were combined and then fractionated by strong cation exchange (SCX).
fragmentation method: Each ion was subjected to Higher energy C trap dissociation (HCD) for fragmentation.
instrument: coupled to Q Exactive™ mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) via a nanospray source.
ionization type: coupled to Q Exactive™ mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) via a nanospray source.
labeling: followed by TMT labeling and LC-MS/MS-based quantitative proteomics studies.
reduction reagent: sample reduction was performed with 5 mM DL-dithiothreitol (DTT) for 30 min at 56°C.
reduction concentration: sample reduction was performed with 5 mM DL-dithiothreitol (DTT) for 30 min at 56°C.
